join_order.note: Semi-join: emit orders rows whose o_custkey is in customer hash set
Equi-join: probe o_orderkey hash map, accumulate SUM(l_extendedprice*(1-l_discount))

filter_pushdown.index: lineitem_shipdate_zonemap — skip blocks where zone.max_val <= 9204 (~35% of 600 blocks skipped)

customer_custkey_hashset.comment: Power-of-2 ≥ 300K/0.75 = 400K → 524288. Maps c_custkey (int32_t) → 1-bit present flag. Built sequentially, probed in parallel orders scan.

customer_bloom_filter.comment: 10 bits × 300K keys = 3MB, fits L3 cache (44MB). Applied before hash set probe during orders scan to skip ~80-90% of non-matching o_custkey values.

orders_orderkey_hashmap.comment: Power-of-2 ≥ 750K/0.75 = 1M → 1048576. Built sequentially from qualifying orders, probed in parallel lineitem scan.

orders_bloom_filter.comment: 10 bits × 750K keys = 7.5MB, fits L3 cache (44MB). Applied before hash map probe during lineitem scan to skip non-matching l_orderkey values.

aggregation.comment: Each thread owns disjoint partitions (hash(l_orderkey) % 64). Contention-free parallel accumulation. ~750K groups across 64 partitions = ~11.7K groups/partition.

topk_heap.comparator: revenue DESC primary, o_orderdate ASC secondary
topk_heap.comment: Priority queue of 10 elements. Each thread maintains local top-10, then merge 64 local heaps into global top-10. O(n log 10) time.

phase_1_customer_scan.reason: 1.5M rows × 4 bytes = 6MB — too small to amortize thread spawn overhead; sequential build of hash set is fine

phase_2_orders_scan.strategy: morsel_driven — divide 150 blocks across 64 threads (zone-map consulted per block)
phase_2_orders_scan.aggregation_note: Each thread appends to a thread-local vector of qualifying (o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority); merge into single list before building orders hash map

phase_3_orders_hashmap_build.reason: 750K entries — fast sequential insert into pre-sized hash map (~10ms)

phase_4_lineitem_scan_and_aggregate.strategy: morsel_driven — 600 blocks, zone-map skips ~210 blocks (max_val <= 9204), ~390 active blocks assigned to 64 threads
phase_4_lineitem_scan_and_aggregate.synchronization: partitioned_no_contention — each thread writes only to its assigned partitions of the aggregation hash map

phase_5_merge_and_topk.strategy: collect 64 thread-local top-10 heaps → merge to global top-10 via partial_sort on 640 elements

index_usage.impact: Reduces lineitem rows to scan from 59.99M to ~39M

index_usage.expected_blocks_skipped: limited (orders not sorted by orderdate) — opportunistic pruning only
index_usage.impact: Minor reduction in orders rows scanned; primary filter is the in-loop predicate o_orderdate < 9204

customer.fused_operations: load c_mktsegment → compare == 1 → if match, load c_custkey → insert into hash set + bloom filter

orders.fused_operations: zone-map check per block → load chunk → filter o_orderdate < 9204 → bloom filter on o_custkey → hash set probe on o_custkey → collect qualifying tuple

lineitem.fused_operations: zone-map check per block → load chunk → filter l_shipdate > 9204 → bloom filter on l_orderkey → hash map probe on l_orderkey → compute revenue = l_extendedprice*(1-l_discount) → accumulate into partitioned agg hash map
lineitem.total_io: ~39M × 24 bytes = 936MB (after zone-map: ~390 active blocks × 100K rows × 24 bytes)

memory_layout.customer_columns: mmap sequential read — L1/L2 prefetch friendly
memory_layout.lineitem_columns: mmap sequential read, parallel morsels — dominant I/O phase

estimated_cost.bottleneck: lineitem sequential I/O (~936MB at ~16GB/s memory bandwidth / 64 threads)

target_comparison.expected_improvement: ~2.7x over current GenDB via: (1) zone-map pruning on lineitem, (2) bloom filter pre-screening for both joins, (3) partitioned parallel aggregation
